text: This is the VOA Special English Technology Report. Kathy Hutchinson is a tetraplegic.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语科技报道。凯西·哈钦森是一名四肢瘫痪患者。

text: She has not been able to move her arms, legs, or speak since suffering a stroke nearly 15 years ago.
zh: 近15年前的一次中风后，她一直无法移动手臂和腿，也无法说话。

text: Recently, she learned how to control a robotic arm using her thoughts.
zh: 最近，她学会了如何用意念控制一只机械臂。

text: She now can use brain activity to serve herself a drink.
zh: 现在她可以利用脑活动来给自己倒饮料喝。

text: The American woman is one of two people who took part in a research project known as BrainGate 2.
zh: 这位美国女性是参与“脑门2号”研究项目的两名受试者之一。

text: The researchers have spent years studying how to help people who are paralyzed regain movement
zh: 研究人员多年来一直在研究如何帮助瘫痪患者恢复

text: in their arms and legs. legs. John Donahue was part of the project.
zh: 手臂和腿部的活动能力。约翰·多纳休是该项目的成员之一。

text: He is a neuroscientist with Brown University and the Department of Veterans Affairs.
zh: 他是布朗大学和美国退伍军人事务部的一名神经科学家。

text: He says people who are paralyzed have their brain disconnected from their body.
zh: 他说，瘫痪患者的大脑与身体之间的联系被切断了。

text: So he and other researchers decided to go around the damaged system.
zh: 因此，他和其他研究人员决定绕过受损的神经系统。

text: They developed a way to go directly from the brain to the outside world.
zh: 他们研发出一种能从大脑直接连接到外部世界的方法。

text: The two paralyzed people had small sensors
zh: 两名瘫痪患者身上植入了小型传感器，

text: connected to the part of the brain that controls movement.
zh: 传感器连接到大脑中控制运动的区域。

text: The devices measured brain activity and sent that information to a computer.
zh: 这些装置能测量脑活动，并将信息传输给计算机。

text: The computer The computer has special software
zh: 这台计算机装有专门的软件，

text: that turns the information into digital commands for operating other devices.
zh: 能将信息转化为操作其他设备的数字指令。

text: The researchers used a highly developed robotic arm to recreate human actions.
zh: 研究人员使用了一只高度先进的机械臂来重现人类的动作。

text: Scientists from Massachusetts General Hospital,
zh: 麻省总医院、

text: Harvard Medical School, and the German Aerospace Center also took part in the study.
zh: 哈佛医学院以及德国航空航天中心的科学家们也参与了这项研究。

text: The BrainGate team had its first success in 2006.
zh: “脑门”团队在2006年取得了首次成功。

text: A tetraplegic stabbing victim was able to use the brain -computer interface system
zh: 一名因被刺伤而瘫痪的患者能够使用脑机接口系统

text: to control a computer cursor.
zh: 来控制电脑光标。

text: John Donahue says the latest development using robotic arms is a major victory.
zh: 约翰·多纳休说，此次利用机械臂取得的最新进展是一项重大胜利。

text: A video of the experiment shows Kathy Hutchinson using such an arm
zh: 一段实验视频显示，凯西·哈钦森用这只机械臂

text: to pick up a cup of coffee.
zh: 拿起了一杯咖啡。

text: She guides the cup toward her mouth, moves it forward, and drinks through a straw.
zh: 她引导杯子靠近嘴边，将其送向前方，然后用吸管喝了起来。

text: The research team carried out almost 200 tests with two different robotic arms.
zh: 研究团队用两种不同的机械臂进行了近200次测试。

text: The two individuals were able to pick up their target objects 43
zh: 这两名受试者成功拿取目标物体的比例达到了43%

text: to 66 percent of the time.
The researchers are calling the information very promising.
zh: 研究人员称这一信息非常令人鼓舞。

text: But they say it will take years to fully develop the technology for everyday use. use.
zh: 但他们表示，要让这项技术完全发展到日常使用还需要数年时间。

text: A report on the Brain Gate study appeared in the scientific journal Nature.
zh: 关于“脑门”研究的报告发表在科学期刊《自然》上。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Persutti.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语，我是卡罗琳·佩尔苏蒂。

text: To read, listen, and learn English with our stories, go to voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: 想阅读、收听并学习我们的英语故事，请访问voaspecialenglish.com。
